item_a.name: EGO Power+ LM2142SP 21-Inch Self-Propelled Lawn Mower
item_a.image_alt: EGO Power+ 21-inch cordless self-propelled lawn mower with battery and charger on grass
pros: Peak Power technology delivers gas-like performance with up to 7.5 ft-lbs of torque
Includes two 5.0Ah batteries for extended runtime up to 60 minutes
Weather-resistant construction (IPX4) allows mowing in light rain
LED headlights enable early morning or evening mowing
cons: Higher price point compared to competitors
Heavier unit at 79 lbs makes transport more difficult
Proprietary battery system limits cross-brand compatibility

40V battery system compatible across 75+ Greenworks tools

Less torque output compared to EGO at 5.0 ft-lbs
No LED headlights for low-light mowing

shop_keywords: Greenworks Greenworks 40V 21-Inch Brushless Self-Propelled Lawn Mower

comparison_summary: EGO brings more muscle with 7.5 ft-lbs of torque and dual batteries that actually last through bigger jobs, while Greenworks counters with a lighter build and that massive 75+ tool ecosystem. Pick EGO if power matters most, Greenworks if you want solid performance without the premium price tag.
verdict_text: EGO Power+ wins for raw power and runtime, making the premium worthwhile if you've got demanding mowing conditions or property over a third of an acre.

key_differences.item_a: Up to 60 minutes on dual batteries
key_differences.item_b: Up to 45 minutes on single battery

key_differences.item_a: IPX4 rated for light rain mowing

seo_content:
I've tested dozens of battery-powered mowers over the years, and the EGO vs Greenworks matchup consistently generates the most questions from readers. These are legitimately two of the best brands in cordless lawn care, but they're built for different types of homeowners.

The EGO Power+ LM2142SP sits at the top of the battery mower market for good reason. That Peak Power technology isn't just marketing—it actually combines output from dual battery ports to deliver 7.5 ft-lbs of torque. That's gas mower territory. I've pushed this thing through overgrown sections of my lawn that would bog down lesser mowers, and it just powers through. The dual 5.0Ah battery setup gives you about 60 minutes of runtime, which translates to roughly half an acre of cutting. EGO also added IPX4 weather resistance and LED headlights, so you can mow in light rain or finish up after sunset.

Greenworks takes a different approach with their 40V model. You're giving up some muscle—5.0 ft-lbs of torque versus EGO's 7.5—but the brushless motor handles regular residential mowing just fine. Where Greenworks really shines is weight. At 63 lbs compared to EGO's 79 lbs, you feel the difference even with self-propulsion engaged. The bigger story is battery compatibility. Greenworks has built out an ecosystem of 75+ tools on the same 40V platform. If you're planning to ditch gas for all your outdoor equipment, that matters.

Lawn size is where you need to be honest with yourself. EGO comfortably handles up to 0.5 acres on those dual batteries. Greenworks works best under 0.35 acres with a single battery. Both give you 21-inch steel decks with comparable height adjustments and mulching options. EGO's 2.5-bushel bag beats Greenworks' 2.0-bushel capacity—not huge, but you'll notice fewer trips to empty on bigger properties.

Build quality leans toward EGO with better weather sealing and heavier construction. Greenworks isn't cheap feeling, just lighter. Both warranties land around 4-5 years, which tells you both companies expect their mowers to last.

The choice really depends on your situation. Need maximum power and runtime for a larger property or thick grass? EGO justifies the extra $200-300. Running a typical suburban lawn under a third of an acre and want to save money while building a compatible tool collection? Greenworks delivers better value.

faq.q: Which has better battery life, EGO or Greenworks?
faq.a: EGO runs longer—up to 60 minutes on dual 5.0Ah batteries compared to Greenworks' 45 minutes on a single 5.0Ah battery. But Greenworks batteries cost less to replace and work with way more tools in their lineup, so there's a trade-off depending on what matters to you.

faq.q: Can EGO and Greenworks mow wet grass?
faq.a: EGO handles it better thanks to IPX4 weather rating, so you can actually mow in light rain or wet conditions without worry. Greenworks can deal with damp grass but doesn't have the same weather sealing, so I'd stick with EGO if you frequently mow in less-than-ideal conditions.

faq.q: Are EGO mowers worth the extra cost over Greenworks?
faq.a: Depends on your lawn. If you're dealing with more than a third of an acre, thick grass, or tough mowing conditions, that extra $200-300 for EGO makes sense. But for typical suburban lawns under 0.3 acres with regular upkeep? Greenworks gives you plenty of performance and saves you money.

faq.q: Which brand has better customer service and warranty?
faq.a: Both offer 4-5 year warranties on mowers and batteries. EGO gets slightly better customer service ratings from what I've seen, but Greenworks has more service centers because they're in more retail chains. So warranty claims might actually be easier with Greenworks depending on where you live.

faq.q: Can I use my existing batteries with these mowers?
faq.a: Only if you're already in that brand's ecosystem. EGO runs on 56V batteries that work with their Power+ tools, while Greenworks uses 40V across their lineup. The batteries don't cross over between brands or with other manufacturers, so you're committing to one system.

faq.q: Is EGO Power+ LM2142SP 21-Inch Self-Propelled Lawn Mower better than Greenworks 40V 21-Inch Brushless Self-Propelled Lawn Mower?
faq.a: It depends on your priorities. The EGO Power+ delivers superior raw power with 7.5 ft-lbs of torque and dual battery runtime that handles demanding mowing jobs better, making it the winner for larger properties or thick grass. However, Greenworks offers solid performance at a lower price point and fits into a massive 75+ tool ecosystem if you're already invested in their platform.

faq.q: Should I buy EGO Power+ LM2142SP 21-Inch Self-Propelled Lawn Mower or Greenworks 40V 21-Inch Brushless Self-Propelled Lawn Mower?
faq.a: Choose EGO if you have a property over a third of an acre or demanding mowing conditions where power and extended runtime justify the premium price. Go with Greenworks if you want reliable mid-range performance, prefer a lighter machine, and prioritize saving money or expanding a compatible tool collection.

faq.q: What is the difference between EGO Power+ LM2142SP 21-Inch Self-Propelled Lawn Mower and Greenworks 40V 21-Inch Brushless Self-Propelled Lawn Mower?
faq.a: The EGO Power+ outperforms with 7.5 ft-lbs of torque and dual battery support for longer runtimes on bigger jobs, while the Greenworks offers a lighter, more maneuverable design and fits into their extensive 75+ tool ecosystem at a lower cost. EGO targets serious homeowners with demanding properties, while Greenworks appeals to budget-conscious buyers seeking solid middle-ground performance.
